schema_rule: The filename holds the one value that is constant within a file (the pulse width); the array axes hold what varies (frequency, time) together with the measured I/Q.

columns.name: pulse_width_ns
columns.description: How long the single drive pulse lasted. One of the two swept variables: 42 values from ~5 ns to ~214 ns, set on the generator as 50 to 2100 clock ticks in steps of 50. CAVEAT: these are converted values that cannot be stored exactly, so use np.isclose() rather than == when filtering.

columns.name: frequency_MHz
columns.description: Drive frequency — the other swept variable. 500 values from 4100 to 4599 MHz in 1 MHz steps. Each frequency gets its own single-pulse ring-down trace. Whole numbers, so == filtering is exact and safe.

columns.name: timestamp_ns
columns.description: Time of the sample within the recorded trace, measured from t = 0 (start of the recorded window). Sample spacing = 1.8084 ns, set by the 552.96 MHz readout clock. All 1000 samples are kept; the trace is never trimmed.

columns.name: I
columns.computed_from: IQ_matrix[0] - loaded directly from the raw .npy
columns.description: Raw in-phase homodyne signal, exactly as acquired (averaged over shots). Note this is the measured signal, distinct from the 30000 a.u. drive amplitude.

columns.name: Q
columns.computed_from: IQ_matrix[1] - loaded directly from the raw .npy
columns.description: Raw quadrature homodyne signal, exactly as acquired (averaged over shots). Combined with I to compute magnitude and phase.
